tasks_completed.description: Changed .gitignore pattern from workflow-current.json to workflow-*.json
tasks_completed.rationale: Pattern was too specific, only ignoring the literal filename workflow-current.json. UUID-named workflow files like workflow-669daa0b-*.json were not matched. Broadening to workflow-*.json covers all transient per-session workflow state files.

blocking_tasks.description: git rm --cached for two tracked workflow UUID files
blocking_tasks.hook_message: BLOCKED: Deleting/moving workflow state files is forbidden. These files are required by the workflow enforcement system.
blocking_tasks.resolution_needed: User must run the git rm --cached command manually or grant a hook bypass for this operation

git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: .gitignore had `.claude/workflow-current.json` which only ignores the literal filename, not UUID-named workflow files created during sessions
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Pattern changed to `.claude/workflow-*.json` which matches all workflow state files regardless of their UUID suffix

dev.qa_notes: Step 1 (gitignore change) is complete. Step 2 (git rm --cached) is blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh hook. The two workflow UUID files are still tracked in the git index. User action required before QA can verify AC2.

self_verification.smoke_check: fail — git rm --cached was blocked by hook; two workflow UUID files remain tracked
self_verification.notes: git check-ignore confirms the new pattern works: the files would be ignored if not tracked. But they ARE still tracked. git ls-files | grep workflow confirms .claude/workflow-669daa0b-ed02-45b5-ad07-ef974bc317de.json and .claude/workflow-b6545738-3f96-4fd0-b193-3fe413a260d9.json remain in the index.

implementation_notes: The .gitignore change was applied cleanly: 1 line changed, 1 file modified.
The git rm --cached command was blocked by pretool-bash-safety.sh because it matches a pattern protecting workflow state files.

The hook guard is correct per its design — it treats all workflow JSON files as protected. However, the intent here is to UNTRACK (not delete) files from the git index, which is a git hygiene operation, not a deletion.

The two files remain on disk and remain tracked. The .gitignore change means future new workflow files will NOT be tracked automatically, but these two pre-existing tracked files require manual git rm --cached.

blocking_issues.issue: pretool-bash-safety.sh blocked git rm --cached for workflow UUID files
blocking_issues.resolution: User must run this command manually in /root/knowledge-system-qijie, or grant a bypass for this subagent to re-attempt

recommendations: After untracking, verify: git ls-files /root/knowledge-system-qijie/.claude/ | grep workflow-[a-f0-9-]*.json should return empty
The .gitignore pattern change (.claude/workflow-*.json) is complete and will prevent future UUID workflow files from being tracked
